content.question: What is the causal direction between school presence and local community welfare in rural Hungary? Does school closure cause depopulation or is closure a response to prior depopulation? The comparative Hungary–Romania study hints at correlation but not causation.
content.reduction_path: A longitudinal quasi-experimental study (difference-in-differences or matched-pairs design) of 20–30 Hungarian municipalities tracking population, employment, and school status from 2000 to 2025, mirroring the Kalaoja et al. Finnish methodology.
